title: Project workspace and review
outcome: A user creates or imports a real local project, asks the direct Codex-backed project agent to change it, receives version-bound file, test, artifact and checkpoint evidence, and can request correction, accept the result or recover without silent data loss.
non_goals: managed background Runs, Action Inbox, Agent Radar and durable background restart, which belong to M03
production Memory Fabric retrieval, multi-device sync, mobile, voice or ambient interaction
providers other than Codex or local models before their adapters and credentials are available
turning the desktop into a full IDE or terminal emulator
force-push, shared-branch merge, release publication or arbitrary remote Git effects without a separately reviewed Effect Claim and permission path
final installer, long soak, hardware fault, backup and broad release qualification, which remain production-hardening work

work_packages.title: Persist privacy-safe local diagnostics
work_packages.outcome: The local development and Personal Quiet profiles retain bounded structured diagnostic logs and lifecycle evidence across process restart, expose a human-readable local diagnostic summary, and prove that correlation survives without recording prompts, responses, credentials or hidden reasoning.
non_goals: making telemetry or debug logs canonical user state
requiring an OpenTelemetry Collector, Grafana or a hosted observability vendor
building the final Diagnostics desktop workspace
collecting raw project files, prompts, model output, secrets, screenshots or hidden reasoning
claiming an exact root cause when the operating system or provider supplies no evidence

reads: privacy-safe tracing fields, component lifecycle and explicit diagnostic context
writes: bounded local diagnostic files, lifecycle markers and derived human-readable summaries outside canonical user memory

required_commands: mise exec -- uv run --project . --frozen python tools/run_in_toolchain.py cargo test -p dennett-observability -p dennett-node -p dennett-head
observability: the package instruments its own startup, rotation, retention, flush and abnormal-previous-exit states
tests inspect serialized output and reject private content, credentials and unregistered high-cardinality fields
rollback: disable file-backed diagnostics and retain console tracing without changing canonical sessions or project data
remove only diagnostic files through their typed retention policy; never delete audit or user data
notes: This is a reliability prerequisite for later filesystem, command and Git mutations, not a production observability platform.
The owner explicitly prioritized reconstructing failures from logs before M02 began.
A mature bounded logging capability is preferred over a custom logging framework; retention and redaction remain Dennett-owned policy.
The lifecycle files are a new pre-release diagnostic schema, so no user-data migration exists yet; changing their private ACL or mode is an explicit permission change.

The diff budget was raised after detached closure review required capability-based filesystem access, non-blocking lifecycle checkpoints, bounded shutdown and adversarial runtime-host race tests; the final increase covers component-wise no-follow root traversal, capability-bound Linux SQLite paths, durable corrupt-sequence recovery, bounded interrupted writes and the closure evidence itself, closing observed reliability and privacy failures rather than adding product scope.

work_packages.title: Define additive project workspace contracts
work_packages.outcome: Additive provider-neutral contracts describe project registration, trust state, workspace identity, snapshots, file changes, command and test receipts, artifacts, checkpoints and review actions without exposing filesystem, Git or Codex implementation types.
non_goals: implementing storage, filesystem, Git, provider or desktop behavior
breaking the accepted M01 protocol epoch
modeling managed Runs or arbitrary external connectors
encoding a full IDE protocol

reads: accepted M01 command, result, watch and error envelopes plus canonical M02 workspace semantics
writes: additive Protobuf sources, generated clients and stable provider-neutral workspace value types

required_commands: mise exec -- uv run --project . --frozen python -m unittest tools.tests.test_protocol_codegen
mise exec -- uv run --project . --frozen python tools/protocol_codegen.py check
observability: every mutating request retains command and correlation identity
error envelopes distinguish stale snapshot, scope denial, conflict, cancellation, missing location and retryable adapter failure
rollback: revert the additive fields and services before a production M02 client ships; preserve the accepted M01 surface unchanged
notes: Generated Rust and TypeScript clients dominate the gross diff budget and must not be hand-edited.
The contract exposes snapshot identity and receipts rather than raw Git CLI or Codex SDK payloads.
DEC-0006 resolved stable project identity, optional portable .dennett identity and shared memory, simple local project policy, relocatable local bindings and the bounded M02 lifecycle.
Cargo.lock is in scope only for the serde_json dev-dependency used by contract serialization tests.

work_packages.title: Own real project registration and trust
work_packages.outcome: The Node durably registers an empty project or existing local folder, resolves canonical location identity, detects Git and instruction sources, presents a bounded trust preview, and restores ready, restricted or missing projects without treating a path or project file as permission authority.
non_goals: deleting source folders or project memory
cloning remote repositories or importing portable memory packs
executing project code before Trusted-Bounded authorization
semantic indexing of project memory
desktop visual implementation beyond transport-neutral fixtures

reads: user-selected location, filesystem metadata, Git identity, instruction manifests and explicit trust choice
writes: Node-owned durable project registry, trust record, instruction fingerprints and system projection

required_commands: mise exec -- uv run --project . --frozen python tools/run_in_toolchain.py cargo test -p dennett-trust-core -p dennett-storage-sqlite -p dennett-node -p dennett-local-ipc
observability: registration, canonicalization, trust decisions, missing-location transitions and instruction fingerprint changes are correlated without logging raw file contents
security-sensitive project changes produce explicit events and actionable safe error codes
rollback: remove a project registration without deleting its folder or session history
roll back the schema only through an explicit export-compatible development migration; never reinterpret an existing registry silently
notes: Project location and trust are durable Node-owned state; the React client never writes paths or grants directly to SQLite.
An empty project creates only the selected directory and one direct session; it does not invent agents or workflows.
Adding an existing folder detects .dennett first and, when it is absent, explicitly offers creation of the minimal portable identity and shared-memory structure without silently modifying the folder.
Ordinary source changes do not reset project trust; project files and instructions can request capabilities but cannot grant or expand local authority.

WP-M02-003 defines the minimal portable structure as .dennett/project.json containing only format_version and project_id plus a tracked .dennett/memory/ container that does not claim to be a valid Memory Fabric pack until its later milestone writes a canonical manifest. It contains no permissions, chats, secrets, provider settings or machine paths.

A plain folder without portable metadata may be rebound automatically only when the Node can prove the same local filesystem identity. A cross-volume copy without portable identity is registered as a new project; M02 does not guess identity from display name or source content.

Invalid or unsupported .dennett metadata is never overwritten by registration. The user may explicitly attach the folder as local-only while leaving that metadata untouched; repair or replacement is a later explicit flow.

Registration uses a durable prepared/filesystem-applied/committed operation keyed by command identity so retry after a partial local folder effect produces one project, one binding and one direct session rather than deleting user files or duplicating state.

The schema-v2 M01 configured project keeps its existing path-derived Project ID as a one-time legacy stable ID so session history is not rewritten. Its current path becomes a binding only when that legacy identity matches; otherwise history remains Missing until explicit rebind, and its initial M02 trust policy is Restricted.

For M02 the Trust-issued decision is a bridge-attested local-user decision: ProjectService accepts it only on an authenticated current client session and authority epoch, with the documented decision kind and an ID equal to the mutating command ID, then persists that evidence with the policy revision. An arbitrary StableRef, project file or model output is not a grant.

WP-M02-003 persists and enforces Restricted versus Trusted-Bounded admission and blocks Missing projects. Exact filesystem, command and Codex effect scopes are enforced and proven by WP-M02-004 through WP-M02-006; this package must not equate stored trust with unrestricted provider access.

Instruction discovery and fingerprint invalidation are completed here. Provider-turn delivery remains a WP-M02-006 conformance obligation because the current runtime request intentionally preserves provider-native workspace instruction loading.
R3 review covers path canonicalization, symlink and junction handling, trust-sensitive changes and migration recovery.

Implementation qualification passed 106 focused Rust tests, 15 targeted Head conversation tests, 59 sequential desktop regressions, two real subscription-backed Codex canaries and the final full repository gate.

A self-audit found that instruction discovery applied its entry count after collecting one directory. The final implementation bounds enumeration while reading and proves incomplete evidence for an oversized tree.

Detached closure review found and verified two repairs: project trust revocation and rebinding are now linearly ordered with provider admission, including a fail-closed constructor path when no registry is installed; portable project metadata publication now uses durable write-through or sync semantics appropriate to the host platform.

The first public CI run exposed two environment-specific assumptions: Linux capability directories can use O_PATH and therefore must be reopened capability-relative before fsync, while the Windows runner may canonicalize its temporary-directory alias differently from the supplied path. The implementation preserves capability-relative durability and the test now compares the admitted workspace with the Node-owned canonical binding.

The implementation is larger than the original 7,500-line review trigger because it includes one durable SQLite state machine, capability-safe folder effects, authenticated transport and adversarial restart/concurrency coverage. The estimate is not rewritten retrospectively; the owner explicitly accepted the internal package and its documented overage after detached closure review, local qualification and green Linux/Windows public CI.

work_packages.title: Control workspace snapshots and file effects
work_packages.outcome: A Node-owned workspace manager pins an exact base snapshot, applies scoped file mutations through claims and receipts, detects concurrent external changes and path escapes, and can compare or restore a checkpoint without overwriting unrelated human work.
non_goals: running project-defined commands or tests
Git remote operations
parallel background Runs
a full filesystem indexer or IDE editor
pretending that already-completed external effects can be undone by a file checkpoint

reads: registered project root, trust grant, current filesystem facts and prior workspace revision
writes: Node-owned workspace leases, snapshot manifests, file effect claims and receipts, checkpoints and conflict state

required_commands: mise exec -- uv run --project . --frozen python tools/run_in_toolchain.py cargo test -p dennett-effect-core -p dennett-node -p dennett-head -p dennett-storage-sqlite
observability: each mutation records project, workspace, base revision, command, effect, target classification and terminal receipt without raw content
stale, escaped, partially applied, cancelled and recovery-required states remain distinguishable after restart
rollback: disable mutation admission while retaining read-only project access and existing checkpoint evidence
restore only files proven to belong to the selected checkpoint and stop on external divergence
notes: One interactive writer may use the selected project workspace; leases and worktrees appear only for real concurrency, risky experiments or explicit isolation.
Snapshot identity is content and metadata evidence, not a promise that every large or ignored file is copied.
Filesystem writes remain local consequential effects even when no remote service is involved.

A mutation is version-bound per touched path: unrelated external edits are preserved and included in the resulting revision, while any touched-path divergence fails before publication with explicit conflict evidence.
Generic file effects never write Git control state or the portable .dennett/project.json identity; Git operations and portable identity changes remain owned by their dedicated reviewed paths.

Multi-file operations persist intent and before/after evidence before publication. A restart may prove completion or leave RecoveryRequired, but cannot infer success or overwrite an unrecognized third state.

Checkpoint restore preflights every touched path against the caller's expected current revision and applies nothing when any of those paths contains a newer unrecognized edit. Already completed external effects remain references only and are never described as undone.
The baseline passed the four-package focused Rust command plus repository and planning verification on 2026-07-22 before semantic edits began.

work_packages.title: Run version-bound project checks and artifacts
work_packages.outcome: Trusted projects run bounded local commands and tests through a supervised Node adapter with streamed output, cancellation and resource limits, while each receipt and collected artifact is tied to the exact workspace revision it verified.
non_goals: running code in Restricted projects
an interactive general-purpose terminal UI
package installation, network access or secret injection without separate grants
managed background Run durability
treating a stale green test as evidence for newer files

reads: trusted workspace revision, exact command request, execution grant and declared artifact collection paths
writes: Node-owned process lifecycle, bounded stdout and stderr evidence, command and test receipts, artifact metadata and terminal state

required_commands: mise exec -- uv run --project . --frozen python tools/run_in_toolchain.py cargo test -p dennett-node -p dennett-head -p dennett-effect-core
observability: command phase, executable identity, bounded argument classification, cwd workspace ID, duration, exit status, cancellation and output truncation are correlated
environment values, secrets and private output are excluded from generic telemetry and retained only as policy-bounded evidence when required
rollback: disable command admission and preserve file review without deleting existing receipts or artifacts
terminate supervised children and mark uncertain termination explicitly rather than claiming cancellation
notes: The first adapter supports the Windows native certification path and deterministic cross-platform fakes.
A test result becomes stale immediately when its bound workspace revision changes.

work_packages.title: Integrate Codex with local Git workspaces
work_packages.outcome: The existing Codex runtime executes against the selected authoritative workspace, emits normalized file and command activity, and uses a local Git adapter for repository discovery, dirty-state evidence, optional isolated worktrees, conflict detection, local commits and explicit cleanup without automatic remote publication.
non_goals: replacing Codex-native sessions, tools, checkpoints or event streams when their semantics satisfy the contract
force push, shared-branch merge, release publication or destructive history rewrite
supporting additional providers or local models
parallel managed Runs
making Git mandatory for ordinary folders

reads: authoritative workspace handle, Git facts, project instructions, runtime controls and active Codex continuation
writes: Node-owned Git/worktree lifecycle and normalized receipts; provider continuation remains owned by the Codex adapter

required_commands: mise exec -- uv run --project . --frozen python tools/run_in_toolchain.py cargo test -p dennett-agent-core -p dennett-node -p dennett-head
observability: one trace joins session, Codex turn, workspace revision, Git base, file effects, test receipts and terminal outcome
worktree creation, dirty base, merge conflict, cleanup and provider failure remain distinguishable without logging file contents
rollback: retain the provider-neutral fake and direct read-only project chat while disabling project mutation through the Codex adapter
keep unresolved worktrees and branches discoverable for manual recovery instead of deleting uncertain work
notes: Single interactive work remains simple; worktrees are required for concurrent writers, risky experiments or explicit isolation, not as bureaucracy around every prompt.
Local commits are reversible project state. Push and pull-request creation are separate consequential remote effects and are not M02 exit requirements.
Live ChatGPT-subscription qualification is manual and never enters the credential-free CI gate.

work_packages.title: Approve the project review workbench design
work_packages.outcome: An editable Figma prototype defines the owner-approved Files, Changes, diff, tests, artifacts and checkpoint review flow using the accepted monochrome Mica shell before substantial renderer implementation begins.
non_goals: implementing backend or renderer behavior
redesigning the accepted Project Chat shell without owner direction
copying generated-image UX semantics
building every future project, artifact or IDE screen

reads: real M02 project, change, test, artifact and checkpoint fixtures plus the accepted M01 visual direction
writes: editable design artifact, deterministic design fixtures and owner-review evidence only

observability: design evidence records fixture, viewport, commit and owner decision without private project content
rollback: discard the unapproved design artifact without changing the accepted M01 renderer or backend contracts
notes: The owner evaluates visual and UX behavior, not implementation layers.
Native Mica behavior is verified later in the Windows application even if the Figma frame represents its visual result.

work_packages.title: Implement truthful project review interactions
work_packages.outcome: The approved desktop workbench creates and imports projects, opens real files, diffs, test receipts, artifacts and checkpoints, lets the user request corrections or invoke guarded accept and rollback commands, and never renders fixture evidence as live state.
non_goals: a full source-code editor, shell or Git client
unapproved redesign of the accepted Mica shell
managed Runs, Inbox or Radar
remote push or pull-request publication
reading local persistence directly from React or Tauri

reads: typed Node snapshots and watch deltas for projects, workspaces, reviews, tests, artifacts and checkpoints
writes: presentation state and stable commands; durable project and review state remains Node or Head owned

observability: every visible mutation invokes a stable command ID and shows pending, committed, stale, failed or recovery-required state
ordinary review decisions are understandable without opening raw logs
rollback: disable M02 mutation commands and retain the accepted M01 chat plus read-only project evidence
notes: Implementation begins only after the owner approves WP-M02-007 visual and interaction behavior.
Right-panel sections remain hidden when no real data exists; placeholder artifacts are forbidden in the native path.

work_packages.title: Qualify and accept M02 project work
work_packages.outcome: One real small repository completes the M02 owner scenario through the native application, while deterministic fault scenarios prove project restore, stale detection, cancellation, partial failure, checkpoint recovery, worktree conflict and privacy-safe diagnostics before milestone acceptance.
non_goals: M03 background Run restart and control surfaces
multi-device, mobile, voice or production Memory Fabric qualification
all-provider conformance beyond the Codex and deterministic fake runtimes
final public installer, broad soak, physical power-loss or release-signing qualification

reads: all accepted M02 contracts, implementation evidence, owner-approved design and a disposable real-project fixture
writes: qualification tests, runbooks, completion evidence, milestone status and publishable M02 blog evidence

required_commands: mise exec -- uv run --project . --frozen python tools/run_in_toolchain.py cargo test --workspace
manual native Windows owner scenario and live Codex project-edit canary
observability: qualification demonstrates one joined privacy-safe trace from owner command through workspace, Codex, file, test, Git and review terminal state
the diagnostic bundle explains injected failures without exposing fixture content or ChatGPT authentication
rollback: hold M02 in QUALIFYING and keep the last accepted M01 public state if any hard gate fails
preserve recoverable workspaces and evidence while disabling unsafe mutation paths
notes: The owner judges whether the ordinary project task, diff and recovery flow is understandable without reading technical logs.
Deeper all-project and long-soak testing remains mandatory before final production release, as requested after M01.
The M02 blog installment is published only after factual, editorial, privacy and independent review gates pass.

owner_acceptance: A user can create an empty project or add an existing folder and reopen it after restarting Dennett
An unfamiliar repository remains Restricted until the user grants Trusted-Bounded access and project instructions cannot expand permissions
A direct Codex-backed chat can change a real file and run a focused check without creating a managed workflow
Every diff, test result, artifact and checkpoint visibly belongs to one exact workspace revision
The user can understand what changed, request a correction, accept the result or recover without reading raw logs
External human edits, missing folders, cancellation, partial command failure and process restart produce honest recoverable states rather than silent overwrite or false completion
Concurrent writers never mutate one shared workspace without explicit ownership or isolation
The Files and Changes experience matches the owner-approved Figma checkpoint and preserves the accepted M01 monochrome Mica shell
